task_completion.description: Agent navigates to an axial slice of the brain showing clear internal structures
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Red slice view (standard axial in Slicer) shows a cross-section of the brain with visible bright white matter and darker gray matter, not a blank screen or scout view.

task_completion.description: Agent places a fiducial point in the White Matter (bright central region)
task_completion.visual_evidence: A markup point (crosshair/dot) is visible in the hyperintense (bright white) central region of the brain tissue (centrum semiovale) in the slice view.

task_completion.description: Agent places a fiducial point in the Gray Matter (darker cortical ribbon)
task_completion.visual_evidence: A markup point is visible in the isointense/hypointense (darker gray) outer ribbon of the brain tissue (cortex), distinct from the bright center and the black background.

task_completion.description: Agent inspects voxel intensity values using Slicer tools
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Data Probe' panel is visible showing intensity values, or the mouse cursor is hovering over the image with intensity values displayed in the bottom status bar or probe window.

task_completion.description: Agent creates a JSON file with the required structure
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal display shows a JSON object containing the keys: 'wm_intensity', 'gm_intensity', 'wm_gm_ratio', and 'quality_assessment'.

task_completion.description: The recorded values in the JSON are anatomically plausible (WM > GM)
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the visible JSON text, the value for 'wm_intensity' is numerically larger than 'gm_intensity', and 'wm_gm_ratio' is > 1.0.

integrity.description: Agent uses the Slicer GUI to derive data rather than guessing
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Slicer application (Markups module, slice views) prior to or during the creation of the output file.
The JSON file is created or edited after the markup points are placed and intensities are inspected, not at the beginning of the session.

integrity.description: Agent relies on image data, not external knowledge bases
integrity.visual_evidence: Agent does not browse the web for 'standard MRI contrast ratios' or copy-paste generic values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: On T1-weighted MRI, White Matter (WM) appears significantly brighter (whiter) than Gray Matter (GM). WM is located in the interior of the brain (e.g., centrum semiovale); GM is the outer cortical layer. A correct WM sample is in the bright center; a correct GM sample is in the darker edge. The WM intensity value must be higher than the GM intensity value.
